text.title: Agent-Builder Community Rituals Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Agent-Builder Community Rituals application must enforce. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Every ritual has a fixed weekly/biweekly/monthly slot in UTC. No 'when we feel like it'. Cancellations announced ≥24h ahead.
rule.rationale: Optional events drift to zero attendance within 8-12 weeks; fixed slots survive.

rule.statement: Every ritual produces ≥1 versioned artefact (eval row added, prompt diff committed, runbook updated). No artefact ⇒ ritual ran in vain.
rule.rationale: Tacit-to-explicit conversion is the point; pure-discussion meetups do not compound.

rule.statement: Public chronological log updated within 48h. Newcomers read before joining the next session.
rule.rationale: Onboarding friction drops 60-80% when newcomers can pre-load context.

rule.statement: Host rotates every 4-6 sessions. Long-running single-host setups burn the host out within 6 months.
rule.rationale: Burnout literature + Eghbal, Working in Public.

rule.statement: Track 90-day retention (members joined → still attending). Target ≥40%. Below 30% triggers ritual-design review.
rule.rationale: Quantitative early-warning; vibes feel fine right up until the community dies.
